lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੂਹੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ਘਰੁ ੧੦ ॥
lines.translit: raag soohee mahalaa 3 ghar 10 |
lines.english: Raag Soohee, Third Mehl, Tenth House:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਾਹੁ ਮੇਰੇ ਸਾਹਿਬਾ ਵਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: vaahu mere saahibaa vaahu |
lines.english: Waaho! Waaho! Hail, hail to my Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕਾ ਨ ਸਾਲਾਹਿ ਜੋ ਮਰਿ ਖਾਕੁ ਥੀਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: lokaa na saalaeh jo mar khaak theeee |1|
lines.english: Do not praise other people; they shall die and turn to dust. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਨੀਆ ਨ ਸਾਲਾਹਿ ਜੋ ਮਰਿ ਵੰਞਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: duneea na saalaeh jo mar vanyasee |
lines.english: Do not praise the world; it shall simply pass away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਹੀਐ ਸਚਾ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sadaa salaaheeai sachaa veparavaahu |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, forever praise the One who is forever True, Independent and Carefree. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਨੀਆ ਕੇਰੀ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਦਝਿ ਮਰੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: duneea keree dosatee manamukh dajh maran |
lines.english: Making worldly friendships, the self-willed manmukhs burn and die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਨਮੁ ਸਕਾਰਥਾ ਸਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਲਗੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh janam sakaarathaa sachai sabad lagan |
lines.english: The lives of the Gurmukhs are fruitful and blessed; they are committed to the True Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਤਮ ਰਾਮੁ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸਿਆ ਸਹਜੇ ਸੁਖਿ ਰਹੰਨਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: aatam raam pragaasiaa sahaje sukh rahan |3|
lines.english: Their souls are illuminated by the Lord, and they dwell in peace and pleasure. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਪੁਰਿ ਬਧੇ ਮਾਰੀਅਹਿ ਵੇਲਾ ਨ ਲਾਹੰਨਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jam pur badhe maareeeh velaa na laahan |2|
lines.english: In the City of Death, they are bound and gagged and beaten; this opportunity shall never come again. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਰਚੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur kaa sabad visaariaa doojai bhaae rachan |
lines.english: Those who forget the Word of the Guru's Shabad are engrossed in the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸਨਾ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਜਲਤ ਫਿਰੰਨਿ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: tisanaa bhukh na utarai anadin jalat firan |4|
lines.english: Their hunger and thirst never leave them, and night and day, they wander around burning. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਸਟਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਨਾਲਿ ਸੰਤਾ ਵੈਰੁ ਕਰੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: dusattaa naal dosatee naal santaa vair karan |
lines.english: Those who make friendships with the wicked, and harbor animosity to the Saints,

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਡੁਬੇ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਿਉ ਸਗਲੇ ਕੁਲ ਡੋਬੰਨਿ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: aap ddube kuttanb siau sagale kul ddoban |5|
lines.english: shall drown with their families, and their entire lineage shall be obliterated. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਹ ਕਾਲੇ ਤਿਨ ਨਿੰਦਕਾ ਨਰਕੇ ਘੋਰਿ ਪਵੰਨਿ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: muh kaale tin nindakaa narake ghor pavan |6|
lines.english: The faces of the slanderers turn black, and they fall into the most horrible hell. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿੰਦਾ ਭਲੀ ਕਿਸੈ ਕੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮੁਗਧ ਕਰੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: nindaa bhalee kisai kee naahee manamukh mugadh karan |
lines.english: It is not good to slander anyone, but the foolish, self-willed manmukhs still do it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਬੀਜਿ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਖਾਵਣਾ ਕਹਣਾ ਕਿਛੂ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: aap beej aape hee khaavanaa kahanaa kichhoo na jaae |7|
lines.english: Whatever you yourself plant, that is what you shall have to eat; nothing else can be said about this. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਮਨ ਜੈਸਾ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਤੈਸਾ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਤੇਹੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: e man jaisaa seveh taisaa hoveh tehe karam kamaae |
lines.english: O mind, as you serve, so do you become, and so are the deeds that you do.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਾ ਪੁਰਖਾ ਕਾ ਬੋਲਣਾ ਹੋਵੈ ਕਿਤੈ ਪਰਥਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: mahaa purakhaa kaa bolanaa hovai kitai parathaae |
lines.english: The speech of the great spiritual beings has a higher purpose.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਭਰੇ ਭਰਪੂਰ ਹਹਿ ਓਨਾ ਤਿਲੁ ਨ ਤਮਾਇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: oe amrit bhare bharapoor heh onaa til na tamaae |8|
lines.english: They are filled to over-flowing with Ambrosial Nectar, and they have absolutely no greed at all. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ਜਿ ਓਨਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹੇ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਲਏਨਿ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: se vaddabhaagee ji onaa mil rahe anadin naam len |9|
lines.english: Those who meet with them are so very fortunate; night and day, they chant the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣਕਾਰੀ ਗੁਣ ਸੰਘਰੈ ਅਵਰਾ ਉਪਦੇਸੇਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: gunakaaree gun sangharai avaraa upadesen |
lines.english: The virtuous accumulate virtue, and teach others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਸੀ ਰਿਜਕੁ ਸੰਬਾਹਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਉਪਾਈ ਮੇਦਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: desee rijak sanbaeh jin upaaee medanee |
lines.english: He who created the Universe, gives sustenance to it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੋ ਹੈ ਦਾਤਾਰੁ ਸਚਾ ਆਪਿ ਧਣੀ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: eko hai daataar sachaa aap dhanee |10|
lines.english: The One Lord alone is the Great Giver. He Himself is the True Master. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸੇ ਮੇਲਿ ਲਏ ਸੋ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸਦਾ ਸਮਾਲਿ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: aape bakhase mel le so prabh sadaa samaal |11|
lines.english: He Himself shall forgive you, and merge you into Himself; forever cherish and contemplate God. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸਚੁ ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਲਿ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਦਰਿ ਨਿਹਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: so sach terai naal hai guramukh nadar nihaal |
lines.english: That True Lord is always with you; the Gurmukh is blessed with His Glance of Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਮੇਲੇ ਤਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਜਲਾਇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: prabh mele taa mil rahai haumai sabad jalaae |12|
lines.english: God merges it into Himself, and then it remains merged; through the Word of His Shabad, the ego is burnt away. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਮੈਲਾ ਸਚੁ ਨਿਰਮਲਾ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: man mailaa sach niramalaa kiau kar miliaa jaae |
lines.english: The mind is impure; only the True Lord is pure. So how can it merge into Him?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸਹੁ ਸਚਾ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਸੰਸਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: so sahu sachaa veesarai dhrig jeevan sansaar |
lines.english: Cursed is the life in this world, of one who forgets her True Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਨਾ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: nadar kare naa veesarai guramatee veechaar |13|
lines.english: The Lord grants His Mercy, and she does not forget Him, if she contemplates the Guru's Teachings. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮੇਲੇ ਤਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹਾ ਸਾਚੁ ਰਖਾ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: satigur mele taa mil rahaa saach rakhaa ur dhaar |
lines.english: The True Guru unites her, and so she remains united with Him, with the True Lord enshrined within her heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਲਿਆ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਵੀਛੁੜੈ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਹੇਤਿ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: miliaa hoe na veechhurrai gur kai het piaar |14|
lines.english: And so united, she will not be separated again; she remains in the love and affection of the Guru. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰੁ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਆਪਣਾ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: pir saalaahee aapanaa gur kai sabad veechaar |
lines.english: I praise my Husband Lord, contemplating the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਲਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸੋਭਾਵੰਤੀ ਨਾਰਿ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: mil preetam sukh paaeaa sobhaavantee naar |15|
lines.english: Meeting with my Beloved, I have found peace; I am His most beautiful and happy soul-bride. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਪੈ ਦੁਧੁ ਪੀਆਈਐ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਿਸੁ ਨਿਕੋਰ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: sapai dudh peeaeeai andar vis nikor |16|
lines.english: Even if the venomous snake is fed on milk, it shall still be filled with poison. ||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮਨੁ ਨ ਭਿਜਈ ਅਤਿ ਮੈਲੇ ਚਿਤਿ ਕਠੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh man na bhijee at maile chit katthor |
lines.english: The mind of the self-willed manmukh is not softened; his consciousness is totally polluted and stone-hearted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aap kare kis aakheeai aape bakhasanahaar |
lines.english: He Himself does - who else should I ask? He Himself is the Forgiving Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਮੈਲੁ ਉਤਰੈ ਤਾ ਸਚੁ ਬਣਿਆ ਸੀਗਾਰੁ ॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: gurasabadee mail utarai taa sach baniaa seegaar |17|
lines.english: Through the Guru's Teachings, filth is washed away, and then, one is embellished with the ornament of Truth. ||17||
